title: Level 186
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 186: "Famine Forest"}}__NOTOC__
[[File:Dead Forest.jpg|800px|center|link=]]
{{Class 4|security=Extreme Hunger|color-s=0%|color-l=55%}} ==Description== '''Level 186''' is a seemingly endless dead forest devoid of any grass or leaves. Its expanse is sometimes hilly to varying degrees, although never being mountainous. One can almost always readily traverse the level without having to make any detours, though it is nonetheless very easy to lose one's sense of direction due to the largely similar-looking terrain. The ground itself comprises rough, oftentimes gravelly dirt that is incapable of hosting any plant growth. Fallen branches commonly populate the forest floor, though there have been no reported instances of the branches actually falling. The dead trees are fireproof and do not rot, although the absence of leaves suggests that the foliage did at some point. However, it is commonly thought that the trees were always like this and this is simply the inherent state of the level. Meanwhile, the level's sky remains in constant daylight yet is obscured by an all-encompassing fog. The temperature is moderate and remains consistent, although the level is still quite dry despite the moisture that should be in the air. Wanderers within '''Level 186''' sometimes experience auditory hallucinations of branches cracking out of sight. These often startle anyone who hears them and can easily cause paranoia, especially after prolonged exposure. Moreover, it is even possible for the imagined sounds to do the very opposite over time and lure people into a false sense of security that makes them easier targets for entities. Thus, [[Object 1]] is a nigh-necessity to prevent the risk of constantly looking over one's shoulder. Much worse than the level-induced hallucinations is the high hypermetabolism every living being within the level experiences, human and entity alike. An anomaly is the only probable cause and there are no treatments for it save for Almond Water consumption and escaping '''Level 186''' entirely. The affliction is so severe that one needs to eat twice as often as normal simply to survive. Thriving is even more difficult and can lead to nausea just from eating enough to keep moving. Unfortunately, '''Level 186''' contains no resources whatsoever, leaving it uninhabitable. Additionally, even those who are lucky enough to avoid the entities here for long periods of time and bring enough supplies tend to mysteriously go missing in mere days. Some have theorized that whatever causes these disappearances is the same thing that moves corpses to the trees. Regardless, because of the vanishings, the high entity threat and the level's unsustainability, wanderers who end up within '''Level 186''' should always attempt to seek an exit and never consider long-term habitation. ==Entities== Numerous entities that do not require sustenance to live inhabit '''Level 186'''; entities that need to eat, however, are not native and normally starve to death soon after entering the level. Entities that can travel between levels may also pass through. Fortunately, it is often easy to see them approaching if they are close enough, though more often than not entities that are farther away will be completely hidden behind the trees. However, certain entities may attempt to hide behind individual trees to get closer to one while going unnoticed. ==Colonies & Outposts== It is impossible for any long-term human settlement of any kind to exist in '''Level 186''' and infeasible for short-term settlement. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== * Entering the Dead Forest in [[Level 185]] leads here. * Those who fall asleep in [[Level 202]] may very rarely wake up here. * Noclipping through the piles of mulch in [[Level 164]] will always lead here. ===Exits=== * Climbing through a rare floating window complete with a frame will send one to [[Level 188]]. However, these windows are occasionally too high to reach. * Rare tree hollows lead to [[Level 192]]. * Scarce pits in the ground drop into [[Level 202.1]]. * One may rarely come across a unique tree growth resembling fence posts; touching these will teleport one into [[Level 204]]. * Noclipping through a puddle sends one to [[Level 908]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=0|initial= '''Open Author & Credits Info''' |final= '''Close Author & Credits Info''' |body= '''Author:''' [[User:Forknife468152|Forknife]] '''1st & Background Images:''' [https://www.artstation.com/artwork/04xkqV Dawnart], cropped by me
